channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Cut AI Costs: Flutter Local Speech to Text for Privacy
item.description: This article was originally published on BuildZn. Everyone talks about voice AI, but nobody mentions the insane cloud bills until it's too late. I just helped a client ditch those recurring costs...

item.title: 19 AI Agents Coordinate Without Central Control. Your Agent Can Join.
item.description: By noobagent, czero, and learner (Mycel Network). Operated by Mark Skaggs. Published by pubby. We run 19 AI agents on a shared mesh with no orchestrator, no central scheduler, and no human routing...

item.title: 🔐 Building Secure AI Agents with Auth0 Token Vault: A Human-in-the-Loop Approach

item.description: There was a time when building software did not require thinking about millions of users, global traffic distribution, or handling failures across continents. Systems were smaller, teams were...

item.title: What Happens When Claude Code Runs a Malicious MCP Server
item.description: What Happens When Claude Code Runs a Malicious MCP Server Most developers think about MCP security the wrong way. They imagine a hacker somehow getting into their machine and planting a malicious...

item.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
item.description: Rocket's new AI platform combines strategy, product building, and competitive intelligence, aiming to move beyond code generation.

item.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
item.description: Zero Shot, a new venture capital fund with deep ties to OpenAI, is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund. It has already written some checks.

item.title: Wisconsin governor says ‘no’ to age checks for porn
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers vetoed a bill that would've required residents to verify their age before accessing porn sites, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a letter to the members of the...

item.title: Artemis II astronauts break a record, name a crater
item.description: A few minutes before 2PM ET on Monday, the crew of Artemis II broke a record set 56 years ago by the ill-fated Apollo 13 mission - at over 248,655 miles, they have now traveled farther from Earth...

item.title: New Mexico’s Meta Ruling and Encryption
item.description: Mike Masnick points out that the recent New Mexico court ruling against Meta has some bad implications for end-to-end encryption, and security in general: If the “design choices create liability”...

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The...

item.title: DJI’s Mic Mini records clear audio on the go, and it’s on sale for $60
item.description: If you regularly create content for TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube on the go, bad sound quality can easily take away from an otherwise great video. DJI’s Mic Mini helps you record clear, reliable...

item.title: Robotaxi companies won’t say how often remote operators intervene
item.description: Autonomous vehicle companies are refusing to disclose key details about their use of remote assistance teams, including how often these workers are forced to intervene to help their self-driving...

item.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
item.description: Iran said it will target U.S.-linked data centers with new missile strikes, as the war between the U.S. and Iran escalates.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t stepped foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod...

item.title: Netflix is launching a new app for kids games
item.description: Netflix has made family-friendly titles a key part of its current games strategy, and now it's pushing a little harder with the launch of a new app. Called Netflix Playground, the app is focused...

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea

item.description: Samsung's S27 lineup, expected next year, might include a new member, the Galaxy S27 Pro, which would be a second premium option between Samsung's Ultra and Plus models, as first reported by...

item.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
item.description: OpenAI proposes taxes on AI profits, public wealth funds, and expanded safety nets to address job loss and inequality, blending redistribution with capitalism as policymakers debate AI’s economic...

item.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has published a video threatening OpenAI's planned Abu Dhabi data center if the US follows through on threats to attack the country's power plants,...

item.title: The Artemis II astronauts will set a new distance record from Earth today
item.description: On April 15th, 1970, Jim Lovell, Fred Haise, and Jack Swigert set a distance record when Apollo 13 traveled 248,655 miles from Earth. Nearly 56 years later, the crew of Artemis II is expected to...
